design_goal: Enable a security admin to configure, test, and launch an SMS phishing simulation campaign — including remediation automation — in under 10 minutes, using a guided wizard that is additive to the existing simulation workflow and not a replacement for it.
user_segments: people manager (receives alerts on repeat failures)
end user / employee (receives simulation and coaching)
constraints: v1 = SMS-only, US-first or limited supported-region beta
Template-based personalization only in v1 (approved library + light customization)

No real credential, OTP, or sensitive data collection at any time — enforced at client layer (no data serialization on submit), server layer (no payload accepted beyond campaign token), and admin UI layer (non-dismissible form safety lock)
Three simulation types in v1: Link only (debrief on click), Credential harvest (simulated login form → debrief), MFA harvest (simulated verification code form → debrief)
Phone numbers stored as hashes, not raw values (phone_hash in data model)
Customer owns legal basis (TCPA/GDPR); Dune provides admin acknowledgment at creation
Architecture must be extensible to WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, QR-code lures, and vishing
Remediation Agent integration is a Must requirement
Test-send before launch is a Must requirement
Phone number sourcing strategy (HRIS, CSV, manual) is Phase 0 — not yet decided
Sender identity (short code, 10DLC, toll-free) not decided
selected_strategy: Dedicated Simulations → Smishing sub-nav section (Option B) with multi-channel-capable data model
selected_strategy_rationale: PRD §8.1 explicitly specifies 'Admin navigates to Simulations → Smishing' as the entry point, indicating a dedicated section rather than a channel selector at wizard start. A dedicated sub-nav makes the smishing campaign list independently accessible and scopes reporting to SMS, enabling a future cross-channel comparison view as an explicit product decision. The underlying campaign data model uses type=smishing within a shared campaign entity that supports future multi-channel types.

alternative_strategies.name: Channel-extended existing wizard (Option A)
alternative_strategies.summary: Add Smishing as a channel type at wizard step 1 alongside Email. All subsequent steps adapt to the selected channel.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: PRD specifies a dedicated Simulations → Smishing navigation path. A channel selector at wizard step 1 works technically but diverges from the PRD-stated navigation intent and makes the smishing campaign list harder to reach independently.

alternative_strategies.name: Channel-first multi-channel wizard (Option C)
alternative_strategies.summary: Redesign the campaign creation wizard to support multiple channels (email + SMS) simultaneously from step 1.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Requires both channels to be production-ready simultaneously; significantly higher v1 complexity; correct long-term direction but wrong v1 scope.

tradeoffs: Admins must navigate to the Smishing section to see SMS campaigns — no unified campaign list until a cross-channel comparison view ships
Phone coverage cold-start: feature is immediately limited by phone number data quality before Phase 0 sourcing decisions are made
Graduated risk scoring UI complexity: five event types require a more complex results table than the current email phishing detail view
assumptions: A 7-step wizard (Audience → Message → Sender+Landing Page → Delivery → Remediation → Test Send → Review+Launch) is the correct structure
Remediation configuration is a dedicated wizard step (not an advanced settings panel) because it is a Must requirement
Test send is a dedicated wizard step because it is a Must requirement and should not be buried in review

Three simulation types ship in v1: Link only, Credential harvest, MFA harvest — each with distinct landing page, events (smishing_link_clicked / smishing_credential_submitted / smishing_mfa_submitted), and remediation rules
The graduated risk model (5–7 event types depending on simulation type) is reflected in the campaign detail view from day one
The compliance acknowledgment checkbox at Step 7 is the correct design for the customer-responsibility legal model
Screen 10 (campaign comparison view) ships as a Should-priority follow-on, not a v1 blocker
open_issues: Approval workflow scope (PM §16.7): RESOLVED — no approval workflow in v1; Launch Campaign is the only CTA at Step 7
Template editability in v1 (PM §16.8): RESOLVED — customer template creation confirmed for v1; full management UI (creation, cloning, editing, archiving) is in scope
Reporting in v1 scope (PM §16.6): the Reported metric card in campaign detail only renders if reporting is confirmed for v1
Sender identity (Eng): short code vs. 10DLC changes the SMS preview sender display in Step 2 and potentially debrief copy

Risk-weighting values (PM/Eng §16.5): confirmed as graduated and separate from email; credential-submitted and MFA-submitted expected to carry higher weight than click-only; values TBD; affects delta magnitude in results but not screen structure
Guardrails for harmful impersonation templates (PM §16.9): credential-harvest and MFA-harvest warnings are designed; emergency/legal/government impersonation guardrail needs content policy decision
Credential/MFA form client-side behavior (Eng): confirm form submit handler fires event + redirect with no data serialization at browser layer; confirm autofill does not trigger smishing event
risks: Phone coverage cold-start: onboarding path for phone data must be visible before first campaign creation

Credential and MFA harvest template safety: design must enforce zero data collection at client layer (no data serialization on submit), server layer (endpoint accepts no payload beyond campaign token), and admin UI layer (non-dismissible form safety lock callout)
Graduated risk scoring complexity: collapsing to a simple click metric would misrepresent PRD intent and undermine behavioral analytics value
new_patterns_required: SMS bubble preview panel in message editor (Step 2) — renders message as a 360px native SMS chat bubble; requires DS review for standardization across SMS and future vishing contexts
Phone coverage progress indicator (Step 1 and Group detail) — coverage bar + count inline with audience selector; requires DS review
Remediation rule-builder cards (Step 5) — if/then toggle cards; may be a new DS pattern if not already standardized in Remediation Agent UI; requires DS review

Mobile debrief landing page — three states: link-only debrief, credential harvest debrief (form acknowledgment + debrief), MFA harvest debrief; entirely new learner-facing surface at 375px; outside admin UI design system; requires documented extension of or departure from Stillsuit DS v2

Graduated stats row (campaign detail) — 5–6 metric cards adapting to simulation type (Link only / Credential harvest / MFA harvest); requires DS review for consistent pattern across email and smishing result views

Template creation full-page form (Screen 1a) — simulation-type-conditional three-section editor (Details / Message / Landing page config); credential and MFA types add form configuration sub-section in Section 3; requires DS review

Simulated login form (credential harvest landing page) — client-only form, no data serialization on submit; fire smishing_credential_submitted event + redirect; requires DS review and Eng confirmation
Simulated MFA verification form (MFA harvest landing page) — single-field OTP form; same client-only smishing_mfa_submitted event pattern; requires DS review
Two-screen mobile preview panel — swipeable form screen + coaching screen preview for credential and MFA templates; requires DS review
next_actions: Design Screen 11 (mobile debrief) — three states now defined (link-only, credential harvest, MFA harvest); no remaining blockers; highest-stakes new surface
Design Screen 1a (Template creation form) — start with simulation type selector and credential/MFA form configuration sub-section; these are the riskiest new patterns requiring DS review
Align with Eng on credential/MFA form client-side behavior (open issue 7) before designing the form back-navigation 'simulation ended' state
Open Group detail view in Figma and map phone coverage tab (Screen 12) — most contained new admin surface, no unresolved blockers
Align with Eng on sender identity before designing SMS preview panel in Step 2 (Screen 3) and Screen 1a
